interpretation: Tier-1 ingest landed cleanly (DB +39 queryable rows, all 5 families ≥ pair_count 5) and did not regress anything. Coverage nudged up (+1 hit@5, r@5 +0.0019). But the primary gate — admission_confused_pairs — did not move (74 → 74). The chosen tier-1 families (Knoevenagel, Hantzsch, Feist-Bénary, Kumada, Dess-Martin) appear to not be the families driving confused_pairs. Corpus-side depth is confirmed neutral-to-positive for these families; compression lever requires different family selection or a different mechanism.
benchmark_runner_environment_fix: All three runners required `conda run --no-capture-output -n chemlens python ...` (NOT direct python.exe). Direct python.exe exits with Windows fatal exception 0xC06D007F (STATUS_PROCEDURE_NOT_FOUND) at numpy import because conda env's DLL directories are not on PATH. Worker .bat files in this directory have the fix applied.
next_step_options: (A) Proceed to tier-2 (families 6-13: Criegee, Curtius, Dakin-West, Davis, Enyne Metathesis, Hunsdiecker, Jacobsen-Katsuki, Charette) with same depth method — may or may not move confused_pairs depending on whether those families drive confusion.
(B) Pivot: extract the actual families driving the 74 confused_pairs from admission/family_admission_confusion.csv and author seed files targeting THOSE families directly.
(C) Accept 74 as the current floor and move on to the workbench lane once example realdata zips (v1-v10+) finish data-ification.
